title: Lumpy German Potato Bake
description: Fatty, crumbly, and nostalgic German dish made with sweet sherry, miso-marinated pork, and a hint of Buddhist temple-friendly spices.
instructions: Peel and dice the potatoes into large chunks.
In a small bowl, mix the miso paste and sweet sherry until smooth.
Cut the pork belly into small pieces and marinate it in the miso paste mixture for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the butter in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 20 minutes.
Add the marinated pork belly to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes, caraway seeds, and black pepper to the skillet. Toss everything together to combine.
Sprinkle the flour over the top of the potatoes and toss again to coat.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the potatoes are golden brown and the pork is cooked through.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley. Serve hot and enjoy!
